name: Action
description: Stories propelled by physical conflict, high-stakes confrontation, and kinetic set pieces, often centered on survival, rescue, or victory over an adversary.

nodes.label: Action Promise
nodes.definition: The audience is promised visceral, high-energy storytelling where physical conflict determines outcomes, protagonists are tested through action, and set pieces deliver escalating excitement.
exit_conditions: Core constraints are established that fulfill this promise
failure_modes: Promise of excitement but delivering only spectacle without stakes
Action without consequence
signals_in_text: Opening establishes the physical world and its dangers
Protagonist's physical capability is demonstrated early

nodes.label: Physical Conflict Must Resolve the Central Problem
nodes.definition: The story's central tension must be resolved through physical action — combat, pursuit, escape, or physical confrontation — not primarily through dialogue, legal process, or internal change.
exit_conditions: Central conflict has a physical dimension that determines the outcome
failure_modes: Conflict resolved entirely through talking or coincidence
Physical scenes are decoration, not decisive
signals_in_text: Victory or defeat depends on what characters physically do

nodes.label: Stakes Must Escalate Through Action
nodes.definition: Each major action sequence must raise the stakes — more danger, higher cost of failure, greater physical commitment — building toward a climactic peak.
exit_conditions: Each successive action sequence is higher-stakes than the last
typical_variants: Personal stakes escalate to collective stakes
failure_modes: Action scenes are interchangeable in intensity
Stakes plateau or decrease mid-story
signals_in_text: Each set piece introduces a new complication or higher danger
Protagonist takes more damage or risk in each encounter

nodes.label: Clear Antagonist or Opposing Force
nodes.definition: The story requires a defined opposition — a villain, hostile force, or dangerous environment — that the protagonist must physically overcome.
exit_conditions: Antagonist or opposing force is visible, active, and dangerous
failure_modes: Antagonist is abstract or absent
Opposition is too easily overcome to create tension
signals_in_text: Direct confrontations between protagonist and antagonist

nodes.label: Protagonist Has Demonstrated Physical Capability
nodes.definition: The protagonist must be established as physically capable enough for the audience to believe they can engage with the threats — through skill, resourcefulness, or determination.
exit_conditions: Audience understands the protagonist's physical abilities and limitations
failure_modes: Protagonist survives through luck alone
Capability is asserted but never demonstrated
signals_in_text: Physical skills shown, not just told

nodes.label: Military/Combat Action
nodes.definition: Action structured around military operations, squad dynamics, and battlefield tactics.
exit_conditions: Action follows military logic — chain of command, tactical thinking, unit cohesion
failure_modes: Military setting but no tactical logic
Action ignores squad dynamics

nodes.label: Chase/Pursuit Action
nodes.definition: Action organized around pursuit — one party chasing another through varied environments, with escalating obstacles and narrowing options.
entry_conditions: Central conflict involves pursuit or escape
exit_conditions: Extended chase or pursuit is the primary action engine
failure_modes: Chase is short and easily resolved
Environment doesn't create meaningful obstacles
signals_in_text: Vehicles, terrain, and obstacles are key

nodes.label: Heist/Infiltration Action
nodes.definition: Action built around planning and executing a complex operation — robbery, infiltration, extraction — where the plan meets reality.
entry_conditions: Central goal involves penetrating a defended space or system
exit_conditions: Story includes planning phase and execution phase with complications
failure_modes: Plan executes perfectly with no complications
No planning shown — action is improvised throughout
signals_in_text: Plan goes wrong and adaptation is required

nodes.label: Survival Action
nodes.definition: Action where the primary goal is staying alive against overwhelming environmental or adversarial threats.
exit_conditions: Survival is the primary measure of success
failure_modes: Survival feels easy — no genuine scarcity or danger
Protagonist has too many resources

nodes.label: Environment Is Actively Dangerous
nodes.definition: The physical environment must present real danger — it's not just a backdrop but an active source of threat, obstacle, or opportunity in action sequences.
exit_conditions: Environment affects action outcomes — terrain, weather, structures create complications
failure_modes: Action occurs in a neutral space that doesn't affect outcomes
Environment is mentioned but doesn't constrain or enable action
signals_in_text: Characters interact with environment during fights

nodes.label: Physical Rules Are Consistent
nodes.definition: The story must establish what is physically possible in its world and then remain consistent — if bulletproof vests exist, they always work; if falls are lethal, they're always lethal.
exit_conditions: Action operates within consistent physical rules that the audience can anticipate
failure_modes: Protagonist survives one fall but identical fall is lethal for antagonist
Weapons and physics change based on plot needs
signals_in_text: Physical limitations are established and respected
Audience can predict what actions will succeed or fail

nodes.label: Weapons and Tools Are Defined
nodes.definition: The story's action toolkit — weapons, vehicles, equipment — must be established and its capabilities and limitations understood by the audience.
exit_conditions: Audience knows what tools the protagonist has and what they can do
failure_modes: New weapons appear from nowhere when needed
Equipment capabilities change based on plot convenience

nodes.label: Opening Action Sequence
nodes.definition: An early action scene that establishes the protagonist's capability, the story's physical rules, and the level of danger — the genre's opening contract with the audience.
exit_conditions: Audience understands the physical stakes and the protagonist's ability to operate within them
typical_variants: Small-scale preview of the central conflict
failure_modes: Opening is purely expository with no action
Opening action is disconnected from the main story
signals_in_text: Physical action in the first 10-15% of the story

nodes.label: Escalation Set Piece
nodes.definition: A mid-story action sequence that raises stakes significantly — introducing new dangers, higher consequences, or greater physical cost than previous encounters.
entry_conditions: Prior action sequences have established a baseline
exit_conditions: Stakes are visibly higher than before — more at risk, more damage, more urgency
failure_modes: Mid-story action is lower intensity than opening
Escalation is merely more of the same rather than qualitatively different
signals_in_text: Protagonist takes real damage or loss

nodes.label: Climactic Physical Confrontation
nodes.definition: The final action sequence where the central conflict is physically resolved — protagonist vs. antagonist, final obstacle overcome, ultimate survival test.
entry_conditions: All escalation has led to this point
exit_conditions: Central physical conflict is decisively resolved
failure_modes: Climax is anticlimactic — over too quickly or resolved by non-physical means
Final confrontation is smaller in scale than mid-story sequences

nodes.label: Kinetic Energy and Momentum
nodes.definition: Action requires a sense of momentum and physical energy — pacing must keep the audience engaged through movement, urgency, and visceral impact.
exit_conditions: Story maintains forward momentum with action sequences at regular intervals
failure_modes: Long stretches without physical tension
Action scenes feel static or choreographed rather than dynamic
signals_in_text: Sensory detail emphasizes impact and movement

nodes.label: Consequence-Free Violence
nodes.definition: Action fails when physical conflict has no consequences — characters shrug off injuries, destruction has no aftermath, and violence is purely aesthetic.
exit_conditions: Violence carries visible consequences — injuries, exhaustion, collateral damage, emotional impact
failure_modes: This node IS the failure mode — its presence indicates a genre violation
signals_in_text: Protagonist takes no damage despite intense combat

edges.label: Promise requires physical conflict resolution
edges.effects_on_stakes: Defines that the central problem must be physically resolved
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist must be capable of physical action
common_alternatives: Some action stories include non-physical subplots but physical resolution remains primary

edges.label: Promise requires escalating stakes
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each action sequence must increase danger and consequence
common_alternatives: Escalation can be in scale, personal stakes, or both
anti_patterns: Flat action — same level of danger throughout

edges.label: Promise requires clear opposition
edges.effects_on_stakes: Defines the source of physical danger
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist has someone or something to fight against
common_alternatives: Antagonist may be human, organizational, environmental, or creature
anti_patterns: No visible opposition — protagonist fights nothing in particular

edges.label: Promise requires capable protagonist
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist's abilities are core to their identity
common_alternatives: Capability can be trained skill, raw determination, or resourcefulness
anti_patterns: Protagonist is helpless throughout — survival is pure luck

edges.label: Promise requires kinetic energy
edges.effects_on_character: Characters are in motion and under pressure
common_alternatives: Pacing may be relentless or rhythmic with recovery periods
anti_patterns: Story is static and contemplative with rare physical activity

edges.label: Physical conflict in military context
preconditions: Physical conflict is military or paramilitary in nature
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes include mission success and unit survival
edges.effects_on_character: Characters operate within military hierarchy and brotherhood
anti_patterns: Military setting but action is purely individual

edges.label: Physical conflict as pursuit
preconditions: Physical conflict involves pursuit or evasion
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes defined by closing or widening distance
edges.effects_on_character: Characters must be resourceful in constantly changing environments
anti_patterns: Chase is brief and resolved without real tension

edges.label: Physical conflict as infiltration/operation
preconditions: Physical conflict involves penetrating defenses or executing a plan
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes include plan success and team survival
edges.effects_on_character: Characters must use specialized skills and adapt when plans fail
anti_patterns: Heist executes perfectly with no physical complications

edges.label: Physical conflict as survival challenge
preconditions: Protagonist is outmatched and must survive
edges.effects_on_stakes: Survival itself is the primary stake
edges.effects_on_character: Character is stripped to essentials — endurance and resourcefulness
anti_patterns: Protagonist has ample resources and safety nets

edges.label: Military context requires dangerous terrain
edges.effects_on_stakes: Battlefield environment adds physical danger beyond enemy forces
edges.effects_on_character: Characters must navigate terrain as well as opposition
anti_patterns: Military action in a featureless, non-threatening environment

edges.label: Chase moves through dangerous environments
edges.effects_on_stakes: Environment provides obstacles and danger during pursuit
edges.effects_on_character: Characters must read and react to changing terrain
anti_patterns: Chase on an empty, straight road with no obstacles

edges.label: Heist requires defined tools and capabilities
edges.effects_on_stakes: Tool limitations create tension during execution
edges.effects_on_character: Characters' specialized skills define their roles
anti_patterns: Unlimited resources remove all tension from the heist

edges.label: Survival demands consistent physical rules
edges.effects_on_stakes: Consistent physics make threats credible and solutions satisfying
edges.effects_on_character: Character must work within real physical constraints
anti_patterns: Physics bend whenever protagonist needs to survive

edges.label: Dangerous environment generates opening action
edges.effects_on_stakes: Opening scene establishes the physical danger level
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist's capability is demonstrated through the opening set piece
common_alternatives: Opening may be small-scale preview or in medias res
anti_patterns: Story opens with extended non-action exposition

edges.label: Consistent rules enable meaningful escalation
preconditions: Physical rules established through earlier action
edges.effects_on_stakes: Audience understands that escalation is real — more danger within established rules
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist faces greater challenge within known physical constraints
anti_patterns: Escalation achieved by breaking previously established rules

edges.label: Tools create escalation when they fail or are lost
edges.effects_on_stakes: Losing tools raises danger; improvising with tools raises tension
edges.effects_on_character: Character must adapt when preferred tools fail
anti_patterns: Same tools used the same way in every sequence

edges.label: Escalation builds to climactic confrontation
preconditions: Stakes have been raised through escalation
edges.effects_on_stakes: Maximum stakes — everything is on the line
edges.effects_on_character: Protagonist must use everything they've learned and endured
common_alternatives: Climax may be single decisive fight or extended battle
anti_patterns: Climax is lower intensity than mid-story escalation

edges.label: Escalation without consequence violates genre
preconditions: Action occurs but has no physical consequences
anti_patterns: This edge IS the anti-pattern — it describes what to avoid
